paper_title: Morphological analysis of pollen grains from heterodynamous stamens of some Aeschynomene L. (Leguminosae - Papilionoideae - Dalbergieae)

methods.description: Process for measuring the polar and equatorial axes, colpus and endoapertures, and exine layers of pollen grains to analyze morphological differences.

steps.description: For each stamen group (+) and (-) of each specimen the polar and equatorial axes in equatorial view were measured in 25 random pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: measure polar and equatorial axes of pollen grains

steps.description: The measurements of length and width of colpus and endoapertures, thickness of sexine layer (tectum + columelae) and nexine layer, total exine (sexine + nexine) and lumen length were based on ten pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: measure colpus, endoapertures, and exine layers of pollen grains

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected pollen grain measurements, including mean calculations, variance analysis, and classification based on base index.

steps.description: For the 25 measurements we performed the arithmetic mean (x), with the standard deviation pattern (sx), the sample deviation standard (s) and the coefficient of variation (V).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate arithmetic mean, standard deviation, and coefficient of variation

steps.description: The comparisons of means were performed through the analysis of confidence interval at 95% of probability, with software Minitab 14.
steps.searchable_summary: perform confidence interval analysis using Minitab 14

steps.description: The values of axes were subjected to variance analysis (ANOVA) and significant differences between means were identified by the Tukey test at 5% probability.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA and Tukey test for significant differences

steps.description: The base index (BX) was calculated and consists of the subtraction of each of the 25 measurements of an axis (MS) by the smallest value of variation range (M).
Classes were established: with values lower than 2.0 considered as a low value (< 2.0 = B-) and values higher than 2.0 as a high value (> 2.0 = B+).
steps.searchable_summary: classify base index values into classes

methods.description: Procedure for obtaining photomicrographs and SEM images of pollen grains for morphological analysis.

steps.description: Photomicrographs were obtained using an OLYMPUS BX 50 microscope with camera and CellSens software for Windows.
steps.searchable_summary: capture photomicrographs using OLYMPUS BX 50 and CellSens

steps.description: For the SEM images the pollen grains were deposited in a metal stub, coated by cathodic spray (Leica EM ACE 600) using Au with thickness of 120 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen grains for SEM imaging with cathodic spray

steps.description: Samples were observed under the SEM JEOL JSM-IT300LV (Tokyo Japan) operating with 20 kV electron beam and the images were digitized.
steps.searchable_summary: capture SEM images using JEOL JSM-IT300LV
